reforms.description: The Canadian government allocated CAD 400 million over three years for the Venture Capital Catalyst Initiative, aimed at increasing late-stage financing for entrepreneurs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases access to late-stage financing, which is crucial for scaling innovative firms and enhancing productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding has been allocated as part of the federal budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant funding aimed at enhancing the venture capital landscape, which is critical for supporting innovative startups.
reforms.source_quote: A positive step in this regard is the CAD 400 million funding over three years for the Venture Capital Catalyst Initiative.

reforms.description: The federal government allocated CAD 950 million to accelerate innovation through five regional 'superclusters', which aims to leverage local strengths for economic growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supports the development of innovation ecosystems that can enhance collaboration and drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding has been allocated to support the establishment of superclusters.
reforms.importance_rationale: A significant investment aimed at fostering regional innovation ecosystems, which can have lasting impacts on local economies.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has also allocated CAD 950 million to accelerate innovation through five regional 'superclusters'.

reforms.description: The 2018 federal budget included CAD 650 million per year in additional funding for research and development, focusing on fundamental research where market failures are most prevalent.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases public investment in fundamental research, addressing market failures and fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding was included in the federal budget and is being allocated.
reforms.importance_rationale: A significant increase in funding for fundamental research, which is essential for long-term innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Around CAD 650 million per year in additional funding for research and development in the 2018 federal budget is focussed on fundamental research.

reforms.description: The Government of Canada will be collecting better data to evaluate interventions in a robust manner, with new funding for this purpose provided in Budget 2018 to Statistics Canada and the Treasury Board Secretariat.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improved data collection will enhance the evaluation of innovation programs, leading to better-targeted and more effective policies that can stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: New funding for data collection was provided in Budget 2018.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards enhancing the effectiveness of innovation programs through better evaluation, which is crucial for informed policy-making.
reforms.source_quote: The Government of Canada will be collecting better data to evaluate interventions in a robust manner, with new funding for this purpose provided in Budget 2018.

reforms.description: The BDC has committed to investing CAD 200 million in women-led technology firms over 2018-19 to 2022-23.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in women-led technology firms is expected to enhance diversity in the startup ecosystem, leading to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: BDC has committed to investing CAD 200 million in women-led ventures.
reforms.importance_rationale: This investment represents a significant commitment to enhancing the startup ecosystem for women-led firms, which can lead to broader economic benefits.
reforms.source_quote: BDC has committed to investing CAD 200 million in women-led technology firms over 2018-19 to 2022-23.

reforms.description: The federal government has prioritized supporting women entrepreneurs through the Women Entrepreneurship Strategy, which includes CAD 1.4 billion in new financing and aims to narrow entrepreneurship gaps.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing access to financing and support for women entrepreneurs, the strategy aims to enhance economic participation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been officially announced with specific funding allocations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant structural change in supporting women in entrepreneurship, which is crucial for economic growth and inclusivity.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has given priority to supporting women entrepreneurs through the Women Entrepreneurship Strategy, which has the potential to bring together scattered existing programmes.

reforms.description: The Canadian government provided additional funding in its 2018 Budget to support the doubling of job placements under the Canada Summer Jobs program, which aims to enhance youth employment opportunities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing job placements, the initiative enhances youth employability and aligns skills with labor market needs, contributing to overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The program was expanded with additional funding in the 2018 Budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of the Canada Summer Jobs program represents a significant investment in youth employment, addressing skills mismatches and enhancing labor market integration.
reforms.source_quote: The government provided additional funding in its 2018 Budget to support doubling of the number of job placements funded under the Canada Summer Jobs program.

reforms.description: The federal government launched the Student Work-Integrated Learning Program to help post-secondary students in STEM and business programs gain work experience, enhancing their employability.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This program aims to bridge the gap between education and employment, equipping students with practical skills that are in demand in the labor market.
reforms.status_evidence: The program was launched to support work-integrated learning for students.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative is a significant step towards enhancing the employability of students in high-demand fields, addressing skills shortages in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government launched the Student Work-Integrated Learning Program to help post-secondary students in STEM and business programs get work experience.

reforms.description: A new Pre-Apprenticeship Program was announced in the 2018 federal Budget targeting underrepresented groups in the trades, including women and Indigenous Peoples.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing access to trades for underrepresented groups, the program aims to enhance workforce diversity and fill skill gaps in the labor market.
reforms.status_evidence: The Pre-Apprenticeship Program was announced to support underrepresented groups in the trades.
reforms.importance_rationale: This program represents a targeted effort to improve access to skilled trades for marginalized groups, which is crucial for addressing labor shortages and promoting inclusivity.
reforms.source_quote: A new Pre-Apprenticeship Program announced in the 2018 federal Budget will target people who are currently underrepresented in the trades.

reforms.description: The Targeted Employment Strategy for Newcomers aims to facilitate foreign-credentials recognition and help immigrants gain Canadian work experience in their profession through improved pre-arrival supports and targeted employment-assistance measures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the recognition of foreign credentials and facilitating work experience, the strategy enhances the employability of newcomers, contributing to overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Targeted Employment Strategy for Newcomers was announced in the 2017 budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a significant effort to improve the integration of newcomers into the labor market, addressing a critical barrier to their economic participation.
reforms.source_quote: The Targeted Employment Strategy for Newcomers announced in the 2017 budget is aimed at facilitating foreign-credentials recognition and helping immigrants to gain Canadian work experience in their profession.

reforms.description: The creation of Offices of the Fairness Commissioner in various provinces aims to ensure that registration practices in regulated professions are transparent and fair, helping to reduce barriers for immigrants seeking to practice their professions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining registration processes and improving support for internationally trained professionals, these offices enhance labor market integration and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ontario OFC has guided changes that removed unnecessary hurdles for applicants through streamlined processes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of these offices represents a significant reform in the regulatory landscape, promoting fairness and transparency in professional registration.
reforms.source_quote: A useful innovation to reduce unwarranted barriers to immigrants registering in licenced professions has been the creation of several Offices of the Fairness Commissioner (OFCs) in various provinces, starting with Ontario in 2006.
